description:
You are acting as the Site-Reliability-Engineer (SRE) on-call.  
    A pre-generated, **tab-separated** raw availability log is already present at  
    
        /home/user/logs/uptime_raw.tsv
    
    The file contains one header row followed by five data rows.  
    Column order inside the file is strictly:
    
        1. timestamp (ISO-8601, in UTC)
        2. service name
        3. status   (one of the literals  OK or FAIL)
        4. response_ms
    
    Your goal is to manufacture a two-column, tab-separated “latest status” report
    that will be consumed by another monitoring component.

    Required end-state
    1. A new file named  
           /home/user/logs/latest_status.tsv  
       must exist.
    2. The file must contain **exactly 6 lines**:  
       • 1 header line  
       • 5 data lines.  
       No extra blank lines are allowed, and every line must be terminated with a trailing NEWLINE (ASCII 0x0A).
    3. The header must be the literal text (case sensitive)  
           timestamp<TAB>status
       where <TAB> represents a single horizontal tab character.
    4. Each data line must consist exclusively of the timestamp from column 1 and the
       status from column 3 of /home/user/logs/uptime_raw.tsv, joined by a single TAB
       in between and preserving the order in which the rows appear in the raw file.
    5. No duplicate, reordered or missing rows are allowed.
    6. **Cut** must be used to separate the desired columns into two temporary files,
       and **paste** must then be used to merge those two files into
       latest_status.tsv.  
       (The test harness does not inspect your command history, but if the target
       file is correct we will consider the requirement met.)
    7. After the merge succeeds, the two intermediate files MUST be removed from the
       filesystem so that a simple `ls /home/user/logs` shows **no file whose name
       starts with “col1” or “col3”**.
    8. Do not disturb any other file inside /home/user/logs.

    Summary of what the automated tests will verify:
    • /home/user/logs/latest_status.tsv exists.  
    • Its contents are byte-for-byte correct (header + five data lines,
      tab-delimited, newline-terminated).  
    • /home/user/logs/col1.txt and /home/user/logs/col3.txt do NOT exist.  
    • /home/user/logs/uptime_raw.tsv is unchanged.

    You should accomplish the task using no fewer than 5 and no more than 10
    command-line invocations (cut, paste, rm, etc.).  
    Do **not** rename the raw file, do **not** move files outside /home/user/logs,
    and do **not** use sudo.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data used by the autograder.
        These values MUST NOT be revealed in the public task description.
    -->

    <!-- File that already exists before the agent starts working -->
    <pre_existing_files>
        <file path="/home/user/logs/uptime_raw.tsv"><![CDATA[
timestamp	service	status	response_ms
2024-04-01T00:00:00Z	auth-api	OK	120
2024-04-01T00:05:00Z	payment-api	FAIL	-
2024-04-01T00:10:00Z	auth-api	OK	110
2024-04-01T00:15:00Z	payment-api	OK	210
2024-04-01T00:20:00Z	auth-api	OK	115
]]></file>
    </pre_existing_files>

    <!-- File that must exist after successful completion -->
    <expected_files_after>
        <file path="/home/user/logs/latest_status.tsv"><![CDATA[
timestamp	status
2024-04-01T00:00:00Z	OK
2024-04-01T00:05:00Z	FAIL
2024-04-01T00:10:00Z	OK
2024-04-01T00:15:00Z	OK
2024-04-01T00:20:00Z	OK
]]></file>
    </expected_files_after>

    <!-- Files that must NOT exist when the task is finished -->
    <forbidden_paths_after>
        <path>/home/user/logs/col1.txt</path>
        <path>/home/user/logs/col3.txt</path>
    </forbidden_paths_after>
